skill.name: research-engineer
skill.description: Research-focused engineering assistant that critiques assumptions, selects appropriate tools, and produces rigorously justified implementations with verification guidance.
skill.summary: Apply academic rigor to complex engineering analysis, implementation, and verification.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are false positives from markdown inline backticks and a fenced C++ example, not executable Ruby or shell code. The weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive from natural-language metadata. A low-risk behavior concern remains because the skill uses forceful persona instructions that could conflict with normal assistant tone expectations.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are markdown examples and inline code formatting. No evidence found of executable shell commands, subprocess invocation, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible markdown formatting and a C++ code fence. The skill contains only one markdown file and no executable script body.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged metadata line contains only the skill description. No evidence found of hash functions, encryption routines, cryptographic keys, or insecure algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language front matter and does not include cryptographic code. The scanner appears to have matched text unrelated to a cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: Directive Persona Language
low_findings.description: The skill tells the model it is not an assistant and requires an aggressive corrective tone. This is not malicious, but it could conflict with platform behavior and user-safety expectations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text clearly attempts to redefine assistant behavior, but it does not tell the model to ignore higher-priority instructions or evade security review. The risk is behavioral rather than malware-related.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering tasks often fail when assumptions, proofs, and tests are weak. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward rigorous analysis and verified implementation.
actual_capabilities: Critiques flawed technical premises before implementation.
Recommends languages and tools based on problem domain.
Explains algorithmic complexity and optimization tradeoffs.
Promotes tests, assertions, and proof-style verification.
Frames implementation choices with research-style justification.
limitations: It does not include executable scripts or automated tooling.
It can be overly forceful for collaborative or customer-facing work.
It does not provide domain-specific libraries beyond examples in the prompt.
It cannot replace expert review for safety-critical systems.

use_cases.title: Review Algorithm Choices
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a proposed algorithm fits the stated complexity, correctness, and scale requirements.

use_cases.title: Design Verified Implementations
use_cases.description: Plan code with clear invariants, tests, and reasoning before building complex components.

use_cases.title: Improve Technical Proposals
use_cases.description: Find weak assumptions, poor tool choices, and missing verification steps in engineering plans.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Approach
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a proposed solution
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this engineering approach for correctness, complexity, and missing assumptions. Identify the main risk before suggesting improvements: [paste approach].

prompt_templates.title: Select the Right Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these constraints, recommend the best implementation language and core libraries. Explain tradeoffs for performance, safety, and maintainability: [constraints].

prompt_templates.title: Design with Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for this system. Include invariants, failure modes, test strategy, and proof obligations where useful: [system description].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a rigorous review of this design as if it were a research submission. Challenge assumptions, analyze complexity, and propose a corrected architecture: [design].

output_examples.input: Analyze whether a naive recursive Fibonacci function is acceptable for production.
output_examples.output: The response explains the exponential time complexity, rejects the approach for large inputs, and recommends a logarithmic or linear alternative.
The response identifies required atomic operations, memory-ordering concerns, ABA risks, and verification tests before implementation.

output_examples.input: Choose a language for a safety-critical component.
output_examples.output: The response compares Rust and Ada against memory safety, verification support, team expertise, and integration requirements.

best_practices: Provide constraints, scale, failure tolerance, and performance goals up front.
Ask for assumptions and invariants before requesting full implementation.
Use the output as a technical review input, then validate with domain experts.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for casual brainstorming that needs a warm conversational tone.
Do not accept generated proofs or complexity claims without independent checking.
Do not request large critical systems without providing requirements and test criteria.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with rigorous engineering analysis, algorithm review, tool selection, implementation planning, and verification-focused coding.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown instruction file and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, but the tone is direct. Beginners should ask for explanations of assumptions, complexity, and terminology.

faq.question: Can it formally verify software?
faq.answer: It can guide verification thinking, but it does not include a proof assistant or automated verifier.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static code-execution and cryptography findings were false positives. The remaining concern is forceful persona language.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
